description_long: This is a round, deep bowl with two large, ornate handles on opposite sides, crafted from a dark, metallic material with a bronze or copper patina. The exterior surface is richly decorated with intricate, raised relief patterns featuring swirling motifs and what appear to be stylized animal or dragon figures, giving it an antique or ceremonial appearance. The bowl sits on a short, circular pedestal base, providing stability and a sense of elevation. The handles are thick, robust, and also adorned with detailed carvings, seamlessly integrated into the bowl's body. The interior is plain and dark, showing signs of age or oxidation, but is otherwise unadorned. The overall craftsmanship suggests it could be a decorative or ritual object, possibly of East Asian or ancient origin.
description: A dark, ornate metal bowl with two large, carved handles and intricate relief patterns of swirling motifs and animals. The bowl sits on a short pedestal and has a weathered, antique finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, ornate handles and the detailed relief patterns encircling the bowl.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle in profile and the continuous decorative motifs wrapping around the bowl.
description_view_2: Rear view with both handles visible, focusing on the symmetry and the uninterrupted relief work.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the other handle in profile and the same intricate surface patterns.
attribute_location: ceremonial space or antique display area
attribute_decorative_patterns: Intricate raised reliefs of swirling motifs and stylized animal or dragon figures encircle the exterior and handles.
